notes: Intel StrongARM successor; ARMv5TE architecture; PXA PDA/smartphone chips; sold to Marvell Jun 2006 for $600M; exited ARM business
32/64-bit RISC CPU architecture; dominant mobile/embedded ISA; basis of Apple Silicon, Qualcomm, MediaTek, NVIDIA Grace; acquired by SoftBank 2016, Nasdaq IPO 2023
Low-power CPUs for PDAs, smartphones, handheld devices; Intel XScale, ARM9/11/Cortex, Samsung S3C, TI OMAP
Samsung 533 MHz ARM920T-based 32-bit RISC mobile SoC; built-in flash boot-loader; camera/LCD/USB/touch-screen interfaces; Windows CE / Palm OS / Symbian / Linux support; 0.13-micron process
Microsoft embedded OS; basis of Pocket PC, Windows Mobile, Windows Phone 7; superseded by Windows Phone 8 (NT kernel) 2012; support ended 2023
Palm handheld operating system; PalmSource spinoff 2003; acquired by Access 2005; last device 2009
Nokia-led mobile OS (Series 60/UIQ); dominant smartphone OS 2000-2010; displaced by iOS and Android; Nokia abandoned Symbian for Windows Phone 2011
